title: betula odorata var. alpestris
intro: Betula odorata var. alpestris, also known as the Alpine Birch, is a variety of the Betula odorata tree, characterized by its distinctive odor and adaptations to high-altitude environments. The medicinal properties of the Alpine Birch include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of the Alpine Birch include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of the Alpine Birch include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or mixing them with carrier oils to create a soothing balm.
